category_title: Students with or at risk for EBD exhibiting off-task, disruptive, or frustrated behaviors
minimal_context: The student is reading a connected text and completing a vocabulary worksheet during core reading instruction.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Please read the next paragraph of the connected text out loud, and then we will fill out the worksheet for the new words.

STUDENT: (Stares at the page, appearing frustrated and confused) I don't know these words. This doesn't make any sense.

TUTOR: Just do your best to read the text.

STUDENT: (Pushes the book away) No, it's stupid. (Starts drawing on the desk instead of reading)

TUTOR: We need to finish reading. Please look back at your book.

STUDENT: (Turns around in the chair, completely off task, and starts poking the student at the next desk) Hey, look at this.

options.A: Establish a norm where disagreement is acceptable and provide a platform for evidence-based exchange of viewpoints.
options.B: Require that each vocabulary worksheet undergoes three revisions, and grade only the final, third draft.
options.C: Purposefully select connected texts written by and/or featuring people of color for reading instruction.
options.D: Use peer-mediated instruction, such as cross-age and same-age peer tutoring during reading instruction.
